name: Jerry Lee Lewis
abstract: Jerry Lee Lewis (September 29, 1935 – October 28, 2022) was an American country music and rock and roll pianist, singer, and songwriter. Nicknamed "The Killer", he was described as "rock 'n' roll's first great wild man".

concerts.label: Chuck Berry and Jerry Lee Lewis
Chuck Berry and Jerry Lee Lewis

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Bennett
about_quotes.evidence: I remember I was twelve years old when I first heard his music... I just grabbed all the recordings of his I could and learned it by ear
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Bennett interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 16, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: I still have – probably have a pretty valuable collection of original 45s, things from Elvis, Jerry Lee Lewis, Chuck Berry, etc.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jang
about_quotes.evidence: I mean I guess Jerry Lee Lewis, I don’t know if you call it dancing but there’s a movement.

about_quotes.speaker: Gap Mangione
about_quotes.evidence: I don’t mean that you’re going to do a Jerry Lee Lewis and dance around.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gap Mangione interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rochester, New York, January 11, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Jamey Aebersold
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Was there a point during this band’s life where pop music like early Elvis, Jerry Lee Lewis, started to creep in...
about_quotes.doc_title: Jamey Aebersold interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 26, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Ousley
about_quotes.evidence: And then Jerry Lee Lewis had “Great Balls of Fire,” a hit out there. So these were the artists on the show.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Ousley interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001
